mnemonics: FINISH: Four Properties (Identity, Inverse), Non-commutative (sometimes), Symmetry (operations), Homomorphism (structure-preserving)
keyPoints: A group is a set equipped with a binary operation that is associative, has an identity element, and every element has an inverse.
The order of a group is the number of elements in its set. The order of an element is the smallest positive integer n such that g^n = e.
Subgroups, cyclic groups, and cosets are fundamental concepts for understanding the structure of a larger group.
commonMistakes: Assuming all groups are abelian (commutative). Many important groups, like permutation groups, are non-abelian.
Forgetting to check closure when determining if a subset is a subgroup. A subset H of G is a subgroup only if it is closed under the group operation and under taking inverses.
Confusing the identity element of a group with the number 0 or 1. The identity is defined by the operation (e.g., 0 for addition, 1 for multiplication).
